task_completion.description: The underexposed landscape image is opened in an image editor (e.g., GIMP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: GIMP or another image editor is open, displaying a dark, underexposed landscape image.

task_completion.description: A color or brightness adjustment tool (such as Levels, Curves, or Brightness/Contrast) is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for 'Levels', 'Curves', or 'Brightness-Contrast' is visible on top of the image editor.
The image canvas shows a landscape that is noticeably brighter and clearer than the initial dark state.

task_completion.description: The brightened image is exported to save the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Image' or 'Save As' dialog is visible, or a file manager shows the newly created brightened image file (e.g., levels_corrected.jpg).

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI of an image editor to adjust the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP's menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) or Python scripts.

integrity.description: The image was genuinely adjusted without extreme clipping.
integrity.visual_evidence: The brightened image retains visible details (trees, sky, water) and is not just a solid white, washed-out, or completely black block.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial image is an underexposed landscape (mean brightness ~48%). The task requires brightening the image by at least 15 percentage points while improving contrast and avoiding extreme clipping (<5% black/white clipping). The intended tool is GIMP 2.10 (specifically the Colors -> Levels tool). Expected output filenames include 'levels_corrected.jpg', 'levels_corrected.png', 'underexposed_landscape_corrected.jpg', or 'corrected_landscape.jpg'.
